title: Ex-Patriots HC Jerod Mayo has a new job away from football
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jerod Mayo becomes managing director of Fifth Down Capital, a private equity firm in Needham.
Mayo previously served as Vice President of Business Development at Optum from 2015-2018.
Mayo was fired as Patriots head coach after a 4-13 season in 2024, ending his football career.

title: Mass. man reports beating Mount Everest speed climbing record
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Tyler Andrews of Massachusetts reportedly broke the Mount Everest speed climbing record with supplemental oxygen, completing the ascent in 9 hours and 55 minutes.
His achievement surpasses the previous record set by Nepali climber Lhakpa Gelu Sherpa in 2003, pending official verification by Nepal's mountain authorities.
Andrews dedicated his climb to friend Greg Krupa, a supporter of The Chaski Foundation, which Andrews co-founded to support emerging athletes and protect mountain environments.

title: You may complain about the T, but a new ranking says it’s one of the best in the U.S.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Travel + Leisure ranked Boston third for best public transportation systems in the U.S., praising its extensive transit options.
Boston's MBTA includes buses and trains, offering convenient access without cars, and serves areas beyond city limits.
Despite recognition, the MBTA faces criticism for delays and disruptions related to weather and infrastructure projects.

title: Wu gets rid of green infrastructure office, lays off director
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Mayor Michelle Wu eliminates Boston's standalone Office of Green Infrastructure, incorporating it into the Streets Cabinet.
Director Max Rome laid off as Office of Green Infrastructure is restructured under fiscal year 2027 budget.
Wu proposes integrating green infrastructure into major street projects to improve coordination and effectiveness citywide.

title: Boston reports uptick in mpox cases
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston Public Health Commission reports an uptick in mpox cases, urging high-risk residents to get vaccinated.
Mpox vaccinations will be available during the Pride flag-raising event at Boston City Hall on June 1.
Symptoms of mpox include rash, fever, and muscle aches; BPHC advises medical attention if symptoms appear.

title: Citgo sign goes dark — temporarily
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Citgo sign in Kenmore Square is temporarily dark as it is being relocated 30 feet higher and 120 feet east.
The move is part of 660 Beacon Street redevelopment, addressing visibility issues caused by new construction nearby.
The relocation, managed by Suffolk Construction, includes two phases and is expected to be completed by December 2026.

title: Canton police respond to 2 ‘significant incidents’ at controversial therapy center
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Canton police responded to two incidents at the Judge Rotenberg Educational Center involving residents and resulting in hospitalizations.
The second incident involved a resident attacking an officer, leading to the officer and two residents being hospitalized.
The Judge Rotenberg Educational Center is the only U.S. facility using electric shock therapy for behavioral issues.

title: Here’s what Boston meteorologists are saying about ‘unsettled weather’ into next week
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Several rounds of showers and gusty northerly winds up to 50 mph expected in Boston from Friday night into Saturday.
A weather alert has been issued for Saturday due to unusually chilly temperatures, rain, and strong winds in coastal areas.
Showers expected from Sunday through mid-week, with a potential mix of wet snowflakes in higher terrain of northern Worcester County.

title: Former home-care aide charged in multimillion-dollar jewelry heist from JP home
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Cory Kisakye, 27, charged with multimillion-dollar jewelry theft from Jamaica Plain home, was arrested in Miami.
Stolen items included diamond rings and luxury watches; total loss estimated in the millions, police report.
Kisakye planned to flee, selling stolen jewelry for cash and purchasing a flight to Uganda.

title: NBA insider’s take on the Celtics-Giannis Antetokounmpo trade rumors
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: NBA insider Brian Windhorst dismisses Celtics-Giannis trade rumors, citing high demands from the Bucks.
Giannis Antetokounmpo, 31, is entering the final year of his contract with a player option for 2027-28.
Antetokounmpo would address Celtics' front court issues after playoff struggles against the 76ers.
